role_title: Senior Product Manager, Project & Task Experience

location.office_time_required: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday in-office standard; Wednesday optional WFH; Friday depends on role

secondary_archetype: Technical PM (enterprise B2B, data models, complex UX)

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in Product Management
Mentor for others; share PM best practices
Base salary range $202,000–$230,000 consistent with Senior IC at a scaled tech company
Expected to handle 'difficult, ambiguous problems where the strategy and/or solution may not be clear from the outset'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in Product Management.
You're a product leader who has the basics of PMing down and can take on difficult, ambiguous problems where the strategy and/or solution may not be clear from the outset.
Systems thinker: You feel just as comfortable modeling data relationships in a technical diagram, just as well as you can partner with Design to walk stakeholders through a holistic UX flow.
You create, flex, and evangelize a roadmap; thoughtfully break down projects to MVPs and iterative projects to ship with high velocity and business impact.
You analyze our top customer personas; dig into the roots of customer needs; synthesize research to gain a deep understanding and narrow definition of a problem.

You partner with cross-functional teammates to deliver high-quality results. You speak with clarity and write sharp product specs. You are highly comfortable getting into deep technical discussions with engineers about the pros and cons of different approaches, and you pair with design on complex user flows.
You are a mentor for others; create great team morale among other functions and PMs; and share PM best practices you've cultivated to date.
Maximize clarity by bringing a data-driven mindset to setting concrete goals and milestones
Define and deliver a compelling roadmap that delivers value for enterprise-level customers

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in building for complex data models and visualizations, human-centric UX, and B2B platform or enterprise experience preferred.
Demonstrates curiosity about AI tools and emerging technologies, with a willingness to learn and leverage them to enhance productivity, collaboration, or decision-making

scope.surface: Project & Task Experience — core Asana project and task product for millions of teams, with focus on enterprise-level customers
primary_metrics_inferred: Enterprise customer value delivered via roadmap
Adoption and usage of project/task features
Shipping velocity and business impact of iterative releases
core_responsibilities: Define and deliver a compelling roadmap that delivers value for enterprise-level customers
Partner closely with Product Design to create an enterprise software experience that's intuitive and dead simple
Create a product strategy to balance multiple priorities and use cases across customers, product teams, and enterprise-level compliance
Maximize clarity by bringing a data-driven mindset to setting concrete goals and milestones
Partner with engineering, design, and other functions to execute on roadmaps with high velocity
Analyze top customer personas; dig into roots of customer needs; synthesize research
Mentor others and share PM best practices

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of compensation package alongside base salary

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($202K–$230K base) and competitive for NYC Senior PM
Explicit encouragement to apply even if not meeting every requirement
Clear product surface and team context (Project & Task Experience)
Annual pay equity audit commitment signals fairness in compensation practices
Named cross-functional partners and specifics of hybrid schedule disclosed upfront
Equity and comprehensive benefits package mentioned
red_flags: NYC office-centric hybrid required (Mon/Tue/Thu); not suitable for remote candidates
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed in JD
Some requirements phrased as behavioral/cultural fit are difficult to objectively assess pre-interview

B2B/enterprise and complex data model experience is listed as 'preferred' in the about-you section but implied as near-required given the enterprise focus of the role — creates ambiguity on true hardness of that requirement

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and 5+ years requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and enterprise focus)
ambiguities: The 'Background in building for complex data models and visualizations, human-centric UX, and B2B platform or enterprise experience preferred' is grammatically grouped with the 5+ years PM requirement bullet but labeled 'preferred' — classified as nice_to_have but is close to hard given the enterprise nature of the role.
Friday WFH is described as dependent on 'the type of work you do and the teams with which you partner' — actual in-office cadence may vary beyond Mon/Tue/Thu baseline.
AI tools curiosity is phrased as a behavioral trait ('demonstrates curiosity') rather than a hard skill, making severity classification ambiguous; treated as soft/nice-to-have.
